original_reasoning: خوث denotes fullness, and ἰχθυώδης means full of fish; the shared core is abundance or being filled.
هدا=calm/quiet (tahdi'a); εὗδε=sleep (imperative). Strong semantic match via sleep/calm
Arabic gharr = crease/fold, ghurar = skin folds; Greek Gerara = place in Palestine. Semitic substrate place name (Gerar in Bible) possibly shared.
Arabic includes being carried or borne; Greek is taking prisoner. Carrying away captives gives a plausible but less direct bridge than حمل.
Arabic is thin-shanked or scant in flesh; Greek spear points are likewise slender and pointed. The bridge is concrete shape.
ملأ/احتوى can drift toward receive or take in, giving a plausible container chain.
Arabic includes protector of the sanctuary; Greek is to have or hold. Protection can arise from holding or keeping, giving a plausible abstract bridge.
visible sword striations can extend to the idea of something plain and evident, hence 'clear'.
